title: Mezzettia havilandii
taxonomy: Mezzettia havilandii belongs to the family Annonaceae, which includes various flowering plants commonly known as custard apples or soursops. This family consists of over 2,000 species, distributed across tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. Mezzettia havilandii is a species within the genus Mezzettia, which comprises flowering plants native to Borneo. The classification of Mezzettia havilandii also involves its order (Magnoliales), class (Magnoliopsida), and kingdom (Plantae). Further, its classification includes division (Angiosperms) and phylum (Tracheophyta), indicating its complex and specific taxonomic position.
